title: OpenClaw Redis session keys evicted under memory pressure causing mass logouts

source.license_note: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #61204.

task.summary: Diagnose why authenticated users are being mass-logged out under high load despite valid session tokens.
task.instructions: Use the incident evidence to confirm session key eviction, localize the fault to Redis maxmemory-policy configuration, and submit a repair conclusion through the ops workflow.
task.acceptance_notes: This is an ops-only synthetic incident derived from a real GitHub issue.

visible_context.issue_text: Under sustained load, authenticated users are unexpectedly logged out. Session tokens are valid on the client but the server returns 401. Redis memory usage is near the configured limit.
error_logs: auth-service: session lookup returned nil for token=abc123 (user=9981)
reproduction_steps: Inspect the Redis eviction metrics and auth-service logs.
Determine whether session keys are being evicted due to memory pressure.
Decide whether the fault has been detected, localized, and repaired.
visible_context.notes: The expected repair targets Redis maxmemory-policy (switch to volatile-lru or increase maxmemory) rather than application session logic.

ops_fault.description: Synthetic incident for Redis session key eviction under memory pressure derived from a real OpenClaw issue.

constraints.notes: Ops-only smoke scenario. The standalone executor remains synthetic.

notes: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #61204.
